notes: Created source-of-truth planning scaffold from ChatGPT consultation. No implementation or verification has started.

Locked final planning refinements: renamed M1 to Verified Ball-Side Block-Shift Evidence Spine, made Gate A/B/C hard promotion gates, selected Python analytical spine plus TypeScript replay, isolated Floodlight behind a parser port, removed spatial control from M1 acceptance, and revised result-count floor. No implementation or verification has started.
Clarified that IDSSE has no public match video, M1-M3 are coordinate replay only, and future video/timecode support is optional and conditional on rights-cleared Priori or provider assets.

Clarified that M4 does not assume Priori SDK/API/private-output access. Without access, M4 is provider adapter readiness with contracts, fixtures, and conformance tests; real Priori integration is conditional.
Re-scoped the project to end at an independent pre-meeting demo. Removed provider adapter readiness and Priori integration from planned work; M4 is now Meeting-Ready Independent Demo Package.

Clarified that M4 is the end of the project as envisioned and must deliver a polished end-to-end vertical slice with delightful UI, smooth coordinate replay, tactical animations, guided demo flow, and visual QA evidence.

Adopted planner recommendation: M1 proves truth, M2 proves query breadth, M3 proves product workflow, M4 is a conditional assistant pilot, M5 proves delight/visual QA, and M6 proves meeting-ready release reliability.

Kept six milestone names but made M4/M5 parallel after M3, made M2 capability catalog mandatory, added second-query-first rule, semantic gold sets, query trace, M4 ship/cut before M6, and no analytical changes once M5 begins.

Downloaded and source-locked the three official Figshare J03WOH raw files. Local files match official sizes and MD5 checksums; SHA-256 values are recorded in the local source manifest. Raw data and generated artifacts are local evidence and are intentionally git-ignored.

Added required verification command entrypoints. Gate A verification currently reports 21 passing source-lock checks, zero failed checks, and six not-ready downstream artifacts: canonical summary, raw parity report, data-quality report, resource report, replay bundle, and replay screenshot.

Implemented the Floodlight-backed Gate A parser boundary, canonical Parquet writer, raw XML parity checks, quality/orientation/resource reports, and a 30-second canonical replay bundle plus screenshot. Generated evidence remains local and git-ignored.
make gate-a-verify passes with 37 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks. Gate A is not accepted yet because independent review and owner acceptance have not happened.

Owner instructed controller to proceed without independent review. Controller review accepted Gate A for proceeding to Gate B with concerns recorded: raw parity is sampled, pitch-plus-5m tolerance must be revisited in Gate B, and later TypeScript replay proof must be stronger.

Provisioned the remaining six official IDSSE matches, canonicalized all seven matches sequentially, and verified corpus invariants. Gate B verification passes with 273 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks.

Controller review accepted Gate B for proceeding to Gate C. Independent review was not performed under the current owner-requested controller-only execution mode. Non-blocking concerns recorded: Floodlight event gameclock warnings, referee tracking excluded from tactical canonical scope, and pitch-plus-5m coordinate tolerance remains a Gate C consideration.

Implemented required tactical primitives, calibrated and froze the query on J03WOH, evaluated the unchanged query on four Fortuna matches, selected 16 accepted real moments and 8 near misses, generated evidence bundles, added the Query 1 semantic gold set, and validated the static TypeScript replay proof. A verifier-caught outcome truncation bug was fixed before acceptance.

Controller review accepted Gate C. make gate-c-verify passes with 304 Python verifier checks and zero failures after the TypeScript replay proof passes with 82 checks. Independent review was not performed under owner-requested controller-only execution mode.
M1 aggregate verification passes across Gate A, Gate B, and Gate C: 37/0/0, 273/0/0, and 304/0/0. This is controller-only verification; final owner acceptance remains pending.

Planner review accepted the architecture but rejected a single oversized M1.1 boundary. Inserted M1.1 Composable Tactical Query Runtime to prove IR, binder, generic executor, relation layer, no-code composition, traces, and M1 parity without Hermes. Inserted M1.2 Grounded Tactical Query Workshop to prove Hermes, feedback, revision diffs, and immutable recipe versions as a bounded client of M1.1. M2 is now the second approved tactical family on top of the runtime.

External planner approved the M1.1/M1.2 split with required changes. Integrated requirements: M1 baseline freeze before implementation, reviewed M1 semantic examples, stronger binder payload/cardinality/unit semantics, tri-state predicates, separated recipe/invocation/bound-plan/execution objects, narrowed geometric_progressive_corridor V1, formal EvaluationTarget non-match handling, scoped Hermes tracking-window access, quantitative M1.2 evaluation corpus, and simple immutable recipe persistence. Implementation remains not started.

Generated the tracked M1 baseline freeze required before M1.1 implementation after the final M1 verifier run. The baseline records verified M1 status, query hash af42853e6a3c8449e023dd98751ababbc7d34640f8751fbffa1c2bbbd0eab523, 16 selected results, 8 near-misses, detector source hashes, evidence bundle hashes, and the explicit boundary that final M1 owner acceptance remains pending while owner baseline-freeze proceed is recorded.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate A. The typed IR defines RecipeDefinition, QueryInvocation, DraftQueryPlan, BoundQueryPlan, and QueryExecution; the binder resolves the frozen M1 plan against a versioned catalog, rejects invalid names, units, cardinality, operators, temporal references, unsupported evidence, and complexity excess, and produces deterministic plan hashes. make m1-1-gate-a-verify passes 47/0/0 after the Gate B strict-gt operator addition. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate C-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate B. The runtime executes the approved bound plan through catalog-ref primitive/operator implementations and matches the full legacy M1 accepted evaluation output: 180 result IDs, classifications, key frames, replay windows, and evidence values. It also matches the 16 selected baseline proof IDs, preserves deterministic repeated execution, verifies selected replay windows against canonical sources, confirms the legacy oracle source hash, and enforces no query/recipe/plan identity branches in the executor. A full-output parity check caught and fixed the wide-entry boundary by adding a strict gt operator. make m1-1-gate-b-verify passes 14/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate C-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate C. The runtime now emits five bound-predicate traces for every accepted result, including status, value, threshold, unit, frame/window, and source evidence. Formal EvaluationTarget inspection reuses the same bound-plan execution path and returns engine-authored failed or unknown predicates for threshold and stoppage non-matches, plus NO_COMPATIBLE_ANCHOR for a quiet forced window. make m1-1-gate-c-verify passes 10/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate D-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate D. geometric_progressive_corridor V1 emits deterministic geometry-only relation episodes from canonical ball, attacking-player, defending-outfield, and orientation data with forward progression, segment length bounds, 5m defender-to-segment clearance, and hysteresis. The relation report records 165 episodes across all four Fortuna evaluation matches and 75 accepted runtime results, includes positive/negative/flicker SVG review cases, and represents UNKNOWN and INVALID relation states explicitly. make m1-1-gate-d-verify passes 11/0/0. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate E-F are implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate E. The external experimental plan reuses the M1 block-shift spine, composes the geometric_progressive_corridor relation as plan data, classifies whether the ball enters the selected opposite-side corridor destination region, and emits 41 experimental results across all four Fortuna evaluation matches: 24 DESTINATION_ENTERED and 17 CORRIDOR_PERSISTED_NO_DESTINATION_ENTRY. make m1-1-gate-e-verify passes 21/0/0, make test passes 20 tests, make m1-1-verify reports 105 pass, 0 fail, and 1 not_ready for Gate F only, and make m1-verify still passes. Full M1.1 remains not_ready until Gate F is implemented.

Implemented and controller-accepted M1.1 Gate F. The static developer inspector exposes a plan selector for the approved M1 plan and experimental opposite-corridor plan, validation summaries, 57 inspectable results, coordinate replay frames, predicate traces, non-match evaluations, replay source paths, and raw evidence values through a generic result contract. make m1-1-gate-f-verify passes 13/0/0.

M1.1 aggregate verification now passes across Gates A-F with 118 passing checks, zero failures, and zero not-ready checks. make test passes 20 tests and make m1-verify still passes M1 Gate A, Gate B, and Gate C. This is controller-only verification; final owner acceptance remains pending.

External review rejected the M1.1 implementation as insufficient proof of composable tactical-query runtime. Controller accepts the rejection as blocking. M1.2 is blocked until M1.1R corrects explicit graph inputs, typed runtime values, invocation semantics, plan-driven classification/evidence, operational unknown handling, relation anchor decoupling, and stronger metamorphic proof gates.
